DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-14-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DANIEL BERKO, Tel No. (415) 771-6174 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Attys. DREXEL A. BRADSHAW and NICOLET E. CORLISS of Bradshaw & Associates, P.C., Tel No. (415) 433-4800 appearing for Defendant Drexel Bradshaw.

RULING - A R G U E D - Defense counsel withdraw their peremptory challenge against Judge HArold Kahn. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT DREXEL BRADSHAW'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT, the court overrules the demurrer to the conversion cause of action. Paragraph 34 of the amended complaint alleges that defendant converted plaintiff's money and the demurrer does not address that theory of liability. Plaintiff is correct that defendant cannot demurrer to part of a cause of action. The court sustains the demurrer to the negligence cause of action to give plaintiff an opportunity to comply with this court's 6/6/14 ruling. The court previously ordered plaintiff to allege the date the representation ended and plaintiff admits that she failed to do so through an oversight. The court rejects defendant's reliance on Lazy Acres Market, Inc. v. Tseng (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 1431. In Lazy Acres, the court dismissed a malpractice cause of action where the client alleged that the attorney failed to advise it of a potential or actual conflict of interest. The court held that there were no facts regarding how that breach caused damage. Here, plaintiff alleges damages. Plaintiff claims that defendant billed her for work that was not done and for work that was adverse to plaintiff. (Amended Complaint Par. 14). In addition, a factual issue remains regarding whether plaintiff would have obtained a better outcome but for defendant's alleged negligence. The court grants plaintiff 10 days leave to amend. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/EHG)

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BRADSHAW AND ASSOCIATES, P.C.'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Third Amended Complaint is denied. "It has long been the rule in California that a party waives any objection to the court's exercise of personal jurisdiction when the party makes a general appearance in the action." (Air Machine Com SRL v. Sup. Ct. (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 414, 419.) (Id. at 420.) "[I]f a defendant seeks any affirmative relief on the merits, the application may be deemed a general appearance." (Dial 800 v. Fesbinder (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 32.) Defendant Bradshaw and Assoc., P.C., generally appeared by moving to compel Plaintiff to produce documents. Defendant appeared in Court and argued in favor of their motion to compel on November 12, 2014 and November 25, 2014, after the Third Amended Complaint was filed. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Off Calendar Feb-11-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT DREXEL BRADSHAW's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT is Denied. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") states that "[e]ach defendant named above is a natural person." (FAC, 4.) CCP sec. 412.30 only applies to actions against corporations. Although the language of CRC 3.221(c) states that a Plaintiff must serve a copy of an ADR package with the complaint, Defendant cites no authority that a Court may quash service for violation of a Rule of Court. Plaintiff is ordered to serve Defendant with an ADR packet in compliance with CRC 3.221(c) within 10 days of entry of this order. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H.
